findings.evidence: `rg` hits: `ClassNumber.lean:107 theorem classNumber_le_of_abs_discr_le_of_finrank_le`, `ClassNumber.lean:122 theorem classNumber_le_nat_of_abs_discr_le_of_finrank_le`, `ClassNumber.lean:131 theorem classNumber_le_of_abs_discr_le`, `ClassNumber.lean:137 theorem classNumber_le_nat_of_abs_discr_le`; the PR proofs call those directly at `ClassNumberNatAbs.lean:55`, `:67`, `:75`, and `:82`, and the exact natAbs forms are obtained by the same theorem with `rw [Int.abs_eq_natAbs]`.
findings.fix: Use the existing `classNumber_le_of_abs_discr_le_of_finrank_le`, `classNumber_le_nat_of_abs_discr_le_of_finrank_le`, `classNumber_le_of_abs_discr_le`, and `classNumber_le_nat_of_abs_discr_le` at call sites with `rw [Int.abs_eq_natAbs]` / `exact_mod_cast`, or keep only a private local bridge where needed.
findings.issue: All new public declarations at lines 51, 60, 71, 78, 85, 93, 100, and 108 are notation/conversion variants of the existing `|discr|` class-number bounds, differing only by rewriting `|NumberField.discr F|` to `(NumberField.discr F).natAbs`.

summary: The new file exposes public natAbs class-number variants that are only parallel spellings of the existing abs-discriminant API after `Int.abs_eq_natAbs`. This should be folded into reuse of the existing theorems instead of adding a duplicate public API.
